proposals.reason: Council and Parliament reached provisional political agreement on 7 May 2026 on the Digital Omnibus on AI — major regulatory event of the week. Confirms Annex III delay to 2 Dec 2027 and Annex I (medical devices) delay to 2 Aug 2028. MDs were not exempted.

card.t: AI Act Digital Omnibus — Political Agreement
card.x: Council + Parliament reached provisional political agreement: Annex III high-risk AI applies from 2 Dec 2027; Annex I (medical devices) from 2 Aug 2028. Medical devices remain in scope; Commission to publish guidance and may limit overlap with sectoral law via implementing acts.

proposals.reason: Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/977 published 5 May 2026 — uniform conformity-assessment / Annex VII rules for Notified Bodies. Operational impact on every MDR/IVDR manufacturer's NB engagement.

card.x: Uniform requirements for conformity assessment and Notified Bodies (Annex VII MDR/IVDR). Tighter and more consistent NB behaviour expected during surveillance audits.

proposals.reason: ISO 14155:2026 (Edition 4) published in March 2026 — applies immediately, no transition. Major rewrite affecting all MDSW manufacturers running clinical investigations. Should be on the timeline.

card.x: Replaces ISO 14155:2020. New requirements for risk management, Clinical Events Committees, Data Monitoring Committees, study design (ICH E9(R1) alignment), adverse event reporting. Applies immediately — no transition period.

proposals.reason: ISO 27701:2025 transformed into a standalone PIMS (no longer an extension to 27001). Certification bodies must transition by 31 Oct 2026; certified orgs by 2028. Important deadline for any MDSW handling EU health data.

card.x: Certification bodies must transition to ISO 27701:2025 (now standalone PIMS) by 31 Oct 2026. Certified organisations have until 2028 to align. Restructured framework mirrors ISO 27001:2022 / ISO 42001:2023 (clauses 4-10).

proposals.reason: Health Canada Pre-market Guidance for Machine Learning-Enabled Medical Devices (PMGMLMD) finalised — official document available 1 Apr 2026. Aligned with FDA/MHRA five-principles PCCP. Relevant to any AI/ML SaMD seeking Canadian market.

card.x: Pre-market Guidance for Machine Learning-Enabled Medical Devices finalised. Lifecycle expectations (data, training, testing, clinical validation, transparency, post-market). Aligned with FDA/MHRA five-principles PCCP.

proposals.reason: FDA Premarket Cybersecurity guidance updated March 2026 — replaces June 2025 version, aligns with QMSR + IMDRF 2020 + closer to MDCG 2019-16. Practical impact on every 510(k) cyber section.

card.x: Replaces June 2025 version. Aligns SBOM and Secure Product Development Framework expectations with QMSR (effective Feb 2026). Closer convergence with IMDRF 2020 and MDCG 2019-16 Rev.1.

proposals.reason: Update existing AI Act 2 Aug 2026 card to reflect that the Digital Omnibus political agreement on 7 May 2026 effectively voided this milestone for high-risk systems. Original date no longer the operational deadline.

card.x: Original AI Act high-risk application date — superseded by 7 May 2026 Digital Omnibus political agreement. High-risk obligations now Annex III: 2 Dec 2027; Annex I (medical devices): 2 Aug 2028. GPAI / prohibited-practice / governance provisions still apply.

proposals.reason: Update existing Annex III Dec 2027 card — no longer 'If Delayed', the delay is now confirmed by the 7 May 2026 political agreement.

card.t: AI Act — Annex III High-Risk Apply
card.x: Confirmed application date for autonomous high-risk AI systems (Annex III) following the 7 May 2026 Digital Omnibus political agreement. Final adoption expected before original Aug 2026 deadline.

proposals.reason: Update existing Annex I Aug 2028 card — no longer 'If Delayed', the delay is now confirmed for AI embedded in medical devices.

card.t: AI Act — Annex I / Medical Devices Apply
card.x: Confirmed application date for AI embedded in regulated Annex I products (medical devices, machinery, toys, lifts, watercraft). Confirmed by the 7 May 2026 Digital Omnibus political agreement. MDs were NOT exempted.

proposals.reason: Update existing 6 May card now that the feedback period is closed — next phase is Council/EP positions. Tighten description.

card.x: Public feedback period on Proposal 2025/0404 closed. Next: Council and Parliament draft their respective positions before trilogue. Revised Rule 11 (default Class I for most MDSW) and AI Act high-risk delineation under negotiation. Earliest adoption late 2026 / 2027.

proposals.reason: Korea's MFDS approved the country's first generative AI medical device (chest X-ray analyser with text summarisation) in April 2026 — operational validation of MFDS Generative AI guidelines (24 Jan 2025). Signals South Korea as fastest jurisdiction for genAI MDSW.

card.t: Korea — First Generative-AI Medical Device Approved
card.x: MFDS grants first approval for a generative AI medical device (chest X-ray analyser with text summarisation). Operational validation of MFDS Generative AI guidelines (Jan 2025). Korea now leading genAI MDSW market access.

proposals.reason: MHRA secured £3.6M (£1.2M/year × 3 years 2026-2029) to expand its AI Airlock regulatory sandbox. Phase 2 closes Spring 2026 (reports Summer 2026); Phase 3 call later in 2026. Major signal UK is positioning as fast-validation jurisdiction for genAI / LLM medical software.

card.x: DHSC allocates £1.2M/year × 3 years (2026-2029). Phase 2 closes Spring 2026 with reports in Summer 2026 (AI diagnostics, PCCPs, LLMs, voice tools, rare-disease diagnostics). Phase 3 design later in 2026.

proposals.reason: On 5 February 2026 the Australian TGA published an updated guidance on AI in medical device software, clarifying that AI is regulated under the existing SaMD framework and does not auto-increase risk class. Important for clients pursuing TGA listings.

card.t: Australia TGA — AI Medical Device Guidance Update
card.x: AI not regulated separately; full SaMD framework applies. Use of AI does not auto-increase risk class. Lifecycle management, change control, clinical evidence and scope-creep obligations apply.

proposals.reason: Two decrees of 21 April 2026 (published in Official Journal) modify the French Code de la santé publique to align the national framework with MDR/IVDR obligations. Direct impact on French market deployment dossiers for Theodo clients.

card.t: France — Public Health Code Aligned with MDR/IVDR
card.x: Two decrees published in the Official Journal modify the Code de la santé publique to align French national framework with MDR/IVDR obligations.

proposals.reason: IMDRF released a draft harmonised technical framework for AI medical device lifecycle management on 6 May 2026 (out of the 29th MC Meeting, Singapore, March 2026). First convergence signal between FDA PCCP, MHRA AI Airlock, Health Canada MLMD, and PMDA IDATEN approaches.

card.t: IMDRF AI Lifecycle Management Framework — Draft
card.x: Draft harmonised technical framework for managing AI-based medical device lifecycles. First convergence signal between FDA PCCP, MHRA AI Airlock, Health Canada MLMD, and PMDA IDATEN.

proposals.reason: MDCG 2025-10 (issued 19 Dec 2025) is the new PMS guidance for MDR/IVDR — shifts from reactive complaint-driven to proactive data-generating PMS systems integrated with QMS. Affects every Theodo client. Missing from data.json — should be in the timeline.

card.x: New EU PMS guidance under MDR/IVDR: shift from reactive to proactive data-generating PMS, integrated into QMS, with PMCF/PMPF, indicators for benefit-risk reassessment, and EUDAMED data sourcing. Mandatory rework of PMS plans.

proposals.reason: New MIR 7.3.1 manufacturer incident report PDF and XSD/XSL files now accepted from 1 May 2026 (SB 11154). Operational vigilance/PMS update — every Theodo client with incidents in pipeline must update their reporting tooling.

card.t: MIR 7.3.1 — Incident Report Forms in Force
card.x: New manufacturer incident report PDF and XSD/XSL files in force from 1 May 2026 (SB 11154). EC published guidance on which MIR 7.3.1 versions are accepted. Operational vigilance update required.
